input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the upper side impact member is highlighted in blue and the lower side impact member is highlighted in pink. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.6.4.4b specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.6.4.4
            TITLE: the upper side impact member must:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.4.4: The Upper Side Impact Member must:
F.6.4.4 The Upper Side Impact Member must: 
a. Connect the Main Hoop and the Front Hoop.  
b. Be entirely in a zone that is parallel to the ground between 240 mm and 320 mm above 
the lowest point of the top surface of the Lower Side Impact Member

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule F.6.4.4b requires verifying that the Upper Side Impact Member (blue) is located between 240 mm and 320 mm above the lowest point of the top surface of the Lower Side Impact Member (pink). The provided engineering drawing only displays horizontal dimensions (309.74 mm and 231.56 mm) and does not include any vertical dimensions necessary to measure the relative heights of the impact members. As per instructions, if a dimension required for compliance checking is not explicitly shown in the engineering drawing, compliance is assumed.

Answer: Yes
